message:
feat(tui): add --reduce-motion flag and REDUCE_MOTION env var support (process_triage-ktge.2)

When enabled, disables toast stagger animation, extends toast display
duration, and skips the periodic tick subscription for static indicators.
Detects REDUCE_MOTION and PT_REDUCE_MOTION environment variables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"process_triage-kipi","title":"Implement Parquet schema stability and versioning tests","description":"## Testing: Parquet Schema Stability

**Purpose**: Ensure telemetry Parquet schemas remain stable across pt versions. Breaking schema changes break analysis tools and historical data.

**Test Categories**:

1. **Schema Fingerprint Tests**:
   - Hash current schema, compare to golden file
   - Detect accidental column additions/removals
   - Detect type changes (int64 → float64 is breaking)

2. **Version Migration Tests**:
   - Read v1 Parquet with v2 code
   - Read v2 Parquet with v1 code (graceful degradation)
   - Column renaming migrations

3. **DuckDB Compatibility Tests**:
   - All schemas queryable by DuckDB
   - View definitions still work after schema change
   - Aggregations produce same results

**Test Implementation**:
```rust
#[test]
fn schema_fingerprint_stable() {
    let schema = telemetry_schema();
    let fingerprint = hash_schema(&schema);
    assert_eq!(fingerprint, include_str!("golden/schema_v1.hash"));
}

#[test]
fn read_v1_with_v2() {
    let old_data = read_parquet("fixtures/telemetry_v1.parquet");
    let new_reader = TelemetryReader::new();
    let result = new_reader.read(old_data);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

**Logging Requirements**:
- Log schema diff on mismatch
- Log column-level compatibility issues
- Include migration path suggestions

**Why This Matters**:
Historical telemetry is valuable. Schema breaks destroy it. These tests catch breaks before release.

**Test Files Needed**:
- golden/schema_v1.hash
- fixtures/telemetry_v1.parquet
- fixtures/telemetry_v2.parquet","notes":"Duplicate/out-of-tree bead.  {"id":"process_triage-ktge","title":"Implement accessibility features (screen reader support, high contrast)","description":"## Overview
Add accessibility features to ensure pt is usable by developers with visual impairments.

## Background
Terminal applications can be accessible with proper design. Screen readers work with text output, and high contrast modes help low-vision users.

## Scope

### 1. Screen Reader Compatibility
- Semantic output structure (headings, lists)
- Alt text for visual indicators (badges → text labels)
- Progress announcements for long operations
- \`--accessible\` flag for verbose text-only mode
- ARIA-like landmarks in output structure

### 2. High Contrast Mode
- \`--high-contrast\` flag
- Environment variable: \`PT_HIGH_CONTRAST=1\`
- Respect \`NO_COLOR\` environment variable
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliant color combinations (4.5:1 contrast ratio)
- Bold text for emphasis instead of color alone

### 3. Keyboard-Only Navigation
- All actions accessible via keyboard
- Focus indicators in TUI (visible, high-contrast)
- Tab order documented and logical
- Escape key always exits current context
- Vim-style navigation (h/j/k/l) as alternative
- Single-key shortcuts documented

### 4. Reduced Motion
- Respect \`REDUCE_MOTION\` preference
- Respect \`prefers-reduced-motion\` (via terminal query if supported)
- Skip animations and spinners when enabled
- Static progress indicators option
- No blinking or flashing elements

### 5. Large Text Support
- Respect terminal font size (no assumptions)
- Layouts work at various font sizes
- Critical info visible without scrolling when possible
- Configurable output verbosity

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/display/accessibility_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 90% for accessibility mode logic
- Test cases:
  - NO_COLOR disables all ANSI codes
  - High contrast mode applies correct colors
  - Accessible mode produces parseable text
  - Reduced motion skips animations
  - Environment variable detection works

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/accessibility_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Full workflow works with --accessible
  - TUI works with --high-contrast
  - Output valid with NO_COLOR=1
  - Keyboard navigation reaches all functions

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/accessibility_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - \`NO_COLOR=1 pt scan\` produces no ANSI codes
  - \`pt scan --accessible\` output readable by screen reader
  - \`pt --high-contrast\` colors meet WCAG AA
  - All commands work with keyboard only
- **Artifact logging**: Output samples for manual review

### Accessibility Audit Tests
- **File**: \`test/a11y_audit.bats\`
- Test cases:
  - No color-only information (verified by grep)
  - Progress has text representation
  - Error messages include context
  - All interactive elements have labels

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`a11y.mode_detect\` | DEBUG | no_color, high_contrast, reduce_motion | Mode detection |
| \`a11y.mode_apply\` | INFO | mode, reason | Accessibility mode applied |
| \`a11y.announce\` | DEBUG | message | Screen reader announcement |

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Detection | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| Terminal type unknown | TERM missing | Use safest defaults | "Unknown terminal. Using accessible defaults." |
| Color detection fails | tput fails | Assume no color | (silent - use no-color) |

### Graceful Degradation
- Unknown terminal → plain text mode
- Can't detect capabilities → most accessible defaults
- User preference always overrides detection

---

## Accessibility Standards
- WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance for color contrast
- Screen reader testing with NVDA, VoiceOver, Orca
- Keyboard-only testing for all workflows
- Documentation for assistive technology setup

## Performance Targets
- Accessibility detection: < 10ms
- No performance difference in accessible mode
- Same functionality in all modes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] \`--accessible\` mode works with screen readers
- [ ] High contrast mode meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 ratio)
- [ ] All features keyboard accessible
- [ ] NO_COLOR respected everywhere
- [ ] REDUCE_MOTION respected
- [ ] Documentation for accessibility setup
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 90%+ coverage
- [ ] E2E tests pass in CI
- [ ] Manual screen reader testing completed

## Dependencies
- TUI mode (process_triage-2ka)
- Shell mode (process_triage-lxiz)
- Depends on: Testing infrastructure (process_triage-aii)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:54:14.863585442Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:55:06.534975680Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":59,"issue_id":"process_triage-ktge","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WCAG color accessibility requirements (contrast ratios, NO_COLOR, high contrast colors, color downgrading) have been absorbed into bd-2hmx (Port theme system to ftui with WCAG Accessibility). The remaining scope of this bead is still valid: screen reader support, --accessible mode, reduced motion, ARIA-like landmarks, and general accessibility audit infrastructure. These features are NOT covered by the ftui migration beads and should be implemented as a separate follow-up.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:55:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"process_triage-kyl","title":"Implement staged action execution protocol","description":"## Task
Implement the protocol for safely executing action plans.

## Background
Section 6 specifies staged execution:
1. Acquire pt lock
2. Revalidate target identity
3. Execute action
4. Verify outcome
5. Log everything
6. Release lock

## Execution Stages
For each action in plan:
1. **Pre-flight**: Verify identity, check permissions, verify not protected
2. **Execute**: Run the action (signal, cgroup op, etc.)
3. **Wait**: Allow action to take effect
4. **Verify**: Check outcome (process state, resource freed)
5. **Log**: Record action and outcome
6. **Handle failure**: Consult recovery tree

## Identity Revalidation
Check immediately before action:
- pid exists
- start_id matches (process is same)
- uid matches (no privilege escalation)
If mismatch: abort and report

## Implementation Notes
- Use tokio for async execution
- Timeouts on all operations
- Graceful cancellation support
- Transaction-like semantics (log intent before action)

## Output Structure
{
  "execution_result": {
    "actions_attempted": 3,
    "actions_succeeded": 2,
    "actions_failed": 1,
    "outcomes": [
      {"target": {...}, "action": "kill", "result": "success", "time_ms": 150},
      {"target": {...}, "action": "kill", "result": "identity_mismatch", "details": "..."
      }
    ]
  }
}

## Deliverables
- Rust module: action/executor.rs
- Staged execution logic
- Identity revalidation
- Unit tests
- Documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Execution enforces stage ordering (pre-flight → execute → wait → verify → log) for every action.
- [ ] Revalidation uses the identity tuple and aborts the specific action (fail-closed) on mismatch.
- [ ] pt lock is acquired before any destructive operation and released on all exit paths.
- [ ] Timeouts/cancellation are supported and produce deterministic failure classifications.
- [ ] Output schema includes per-action results with timings and failure types.

## Test Plan
- Unit: identity mismatch, permission denied, timeout, cancellation paths.
- Integration: lock contention (daemon/manual), supervised respawn detection surfaced to recovery tree.
- E2E: apply a small plan against fixture processes and verify before/after outcomes + logs.
- Logging: assert executor logs intent, attempt, result, and verification for each step.
","notes":"Implemented staged action execution protocol scaffolding: added crates/pt-core/src/action/executor.rs with ActionExecutor (lock acquisition, preflight identity check, execute+verify stages), ActionResult/ExecutionResult, ActionRunner + IdentityProvider traits, and tests for identity mismatch and lock contention. Added crates/pt-core/src/action/mod.rs and exported in crates/pt-core/src/lib.rs. Ran: cargo test -p pt-core (warnings about existing assert_cmd deprecation + dead_code).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:29:48.779315736Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T14:50:23.658098368Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T14:50:23.658104910Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-kyl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-1t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-kyl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-sj6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-kyy9","title":"E2E: Container and GPU process detection tests","description":"## Overview
Test suite for container-aware process grouping and GPU process detection features.

## Scope

### 1. Container Detection Tests
- cgroup v1 path parsing (Docker, systemd)
- cgroup v2 path parsing (unified hierarchy)
- Container ID extraction for Docker, containerd, CRI-O, podman
- Kubernetes namespace/pod/container extraction
- Process grouping by container

### 2. Container Integration Tests (Docker required)
- Start test containers, detect processes
- Container filter flags (--container, --pod)
- Blast radius includes container scope
- Container restart action (mock execution)

### 3. GPU Detection Tests
- nvidia-smi output parsing (various versions)
- rocm-smi output parsing
- GPU PID to process mapping
- VRAM usage calculation
- Multi-GPU handling

### 4. GPU Integration Tests (GPU or mock)
- --gpu filter for GPU processes
- GPU evidence in detailed view
- Stuck GPU process detection patterns
- Post-kill VRAM verification (mock)

### 5. Mock Tests
- Mocked cgroup files for deterministic testing
- Mocked nvidia-smi responses
- Error handling: tools unavailable
- Graceful degradation verification

## Test Files
- \`crates/pt-core/tests/container_detection.rs\`: Container unit tests
- \`crates/pt-core/tests/gpu_detection.rs\`: GPU unit tests
- \`test/container_e2e.bats\`: Container E2E (Docker required)
- \`test/gpu_e2e.bats\`: GPU E2E (GPU or mock)
- \`crates/pt-core/tests/container_mock.rs\`: Mock tests
- \`crates/pt-core/tests/gpu_mock.rs\`: Mock tests

## Artifact Logging
- cgroup file samples
- nvidia-smi output samples
- Container/process mapping
- Detection timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Container detection tests pass (mock and real)
- [ ] Kubernetes pod extraction works
- [ ] GPU detection tests pass (mock and real if available)
- [ ] Graceful degradation when tools unavailable
- [ ] E2E tests with Docker in CI","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T20:36:14.504936696Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T10:06:25.149320115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T10:06:25.149257949Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-kyy9","depends_on_id":"process_triage-7bfx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-kyy9","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-kyy9","depends_on_id":"process_triage-g0lz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-kze","title":"Define package architecture: pt wrapper vs pt-core monolith","description":"## Task
Define the clear boundary between:
- **pt (bash wrapper/installer)**: Handles installation, capability discovery, environment detection, and launches pt-core
- **pt-core (Rust monolith)**: The actual implementation containing scan/deep-scan/infer/decide/ui/agent/duck/bundle/report/daemon

## Background
The plan calls for a two-tier architecture where bash handles the messy platform-specific installation and capability discovery, while Rust handles the computationally intensive inference and UX. This separation:
1. Allows the wrapper to degrade gracefully on any platform
2. Keeps the core focused and testable
3. Enables the core to assume capabilities are known (passed via manifest)

## Deliverables
- Document which responsibilities belong to pt (bash)
- Document which responsibilities belong to pt-core (Rust)
- Define the interface between them (capabilities manifest format)
- Define version coordination between wrapper and core

## Technical Considerations
- Wrapper must detect OS, package manager, available tools
- Wrapper should attempt maximal tool installation (see Phase 3a)
- Wrapper passes capabilities manifest to core via CLI flag or env
- Core should have a standalone mode for testing without wrapper

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear specification document exists
- Capabilities manifest schema is defined
- Both teams (if multiple) can work independently against the interface","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:20:22.103055986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T13:44:15.697631111Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T13:44:15.697631111Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created specs/package-architecture.md and specs/schemas/capabilities-manifest.schema.json defining the two-tier architecture (pt bash wrapper + pt-core Rust monolith), capabilities manifest schema v1.0.0, exit codes, version coordination, and interface contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-kze","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2l3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
